seoTitle: Downside vs Doyalson North — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Downside and Doyalson North. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Downside vs Doyalson North: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Downside and Doyalson North.
heading: Downside vs Doyalson North — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Downside, New South Wales and Doyalson North, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Downside has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Doyalson North (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Downside or Doyalson North safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Downside has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Doyalson North scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Downside scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Doyalson North scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Downside or Doyalson North?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Downside has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
